news.title: Haryana: HSPCB notice to HSVP over illegal C&D waste dumping
news.para: The Haryana State Pollution Control Board has issued a show-cause notice to the Haryana Shehri Vikas Pradhikaran for illegally dumping construction and demolition waste on HSVP land in Sector 5. The board cited violations of environmental rules, leading to pollution and health hazards, and demanded immediate removal of the waste.

news.title: Prepare list of illegal buildings: West Bengal municipal affairs minister to civic bodies
news.para: Following a fatal factory fire, the urban development and municipal affairs department has directed all civic bodies to strictly enforce laws against unauthorized construction. Municipalities are required to report on illegal buildings and the actions taken to prevent them. Notices will be issued to violators, with potential imprisonment and fines for non-compliance.

news.title: Relief for Ulhasnagar residents as state starts to regularise homes
news.para: Thousands of Ulhasnagar families may get legal ownership of homes built before 2011. The Maharashtra government has started a major regularisation process. A special review committee is formed to speed up approvals. This move offers relief to lower and middle-income families living in uncertainty for years. Legal recognition will provide dignity, security, and access to civic amenities.

news.title: Delhi development body picks TOD plots to push affordable housing
news.para: Delhi Development Authority has identified 14 land parcels along metro corridors for affordable housing and mixed-use development. These sites, totaling over 3.6 lakh square meters, aim to promote Transit-Oriented Development (TOD) and curb outward migration. A portal will be created for developers to submit proposals under the new policy.

news.title: Nagpur civic body eyes only ₹749 crore property tax for FY27
news.para: Nagpur Municipal Corporation set a Rs749.82 crore property tax collection target for 2026-27, but excluded a staggering Rs1,392 crore in arrears from its recovery plan. This leaves nearly 2.5 times the target amount unaddressed, raising concerns about selective enforcement and weak financial planning.

news.title: Act against builders on groundwater misuse, CGWA tells Uttar Pradesh government
news.para: The Central Groundwater Authority has directed the Uttar Pradesh groundwater department to take action against real estate developers in Noida. This follows a complaint alleging excessive groundwater extraction and wastage at construction sites. Water pumped out during basement excavation is reportedly being discharged into drains. The authority has requested a report on the actions taken.

news.title: Land auctions in Prayagraj now open to buyers across India, abroad
news.para: The Prayagraj Development Authority has launched its first online land auction system, allowing nationwide and international participation in land transactions. This digital platform aims to enhance transparency and convenience for builders and investors, with plots worth approximately Rs 300 crore already listed.

news.title: Only five RERA-registered JDA projects involve non-agriculturist partners: Himachal Pradesh RERA
news.para: The clarification was issued by HPRERA Chairperson RD Dhiman in response to queries raised by the Housing Department regarding housing projects executed under Joint Development Agreements (JDAs).

news.title: Secunderabad: RWAs recommend CCTV, security audits & staff verification in gated communities
news.para: Resident welfare associations met with Secunderabad police to discuss enhanced safety for women and seniors while respecting domestic workers' dignity. Recommendations included mandatory police verification, digital gate systems, and CCTV surveillance. Police highlighted ongoing safety initiatives and urged community cooperation in awareness programs.

news.title: Over 13.25 lakh sq yards freed across Noida in 2025-26
news.para: Gautam Buddh Nagar police have reclaimed over 13 lakh square yards of encroached land, valued at more than Rs 5,900 crore, in a major anti-encroachment drive. The campaign, targeting land mafias and illegal structures, aims to facilitate planned urban development and public welfare projects. Strict legal action has been taken against offenders, with the drive set to continue.

news.title: Nagpur: Only 15% of public reservations from DP-2000 implemented over 25 years
news.para: Nagpur's Existing Land Use exercise revealed a significant urban planning failure, with only 15% of 893 reserved plots implemented over 25 years. Many reservations were reduced, redesignated, or encroached upon, leading to shrinking open spaces and strained infrastructure. This data will inform the revised Development Plan, aiming to address past shortcomings.

news.title: Gurugram residents move NGT against proposed C&D waste collection centre
news.para: The plea has sought urgent intervention from the tribunal to restrain MCG from proceeding with the proposed facility and to remove structures already constructed at the site

news.title: Tamil Nadu RWA alliance urges statewide audit of property tax evasion
news.para: Resident welfare associations in Tamil Nadu are demanding a statewide audit of buildings. They allege widespread property tax evasion and corrupt assessment practices are costing the state hundreds of crores. The federation seeks inspections of properties owned by political figures and strict action against evaders and negligent officials.

news.title: Mysuru development body plans auction of reclaimed land
news.para: The Mysuru Development Authority has recovered approximately 150 acres of encroached land. This land, located in developed areas like Vijayanagar, was acquired decades ago. The authority now plans to develop these reclaimed properties into residential sites. These sites will then be put up for auction.

news.title: Delhi high street retailers push NDMC to rework property tax
news.para: At the New Delhi Municipal Corporation (NDMC) under which these markets come, the property tax is up to 25% of the rent collected while in other parts of Delhi it's just 2-3%.

news.title: Bengaluru development body’s housing trouble reaches breaking point
news.para: K-RERA, while hearing a petition filed by Narayan Shetty, one of the allottees, stated that Phases 1 and 2 of the project are ongoing projects requiring regulation under Section 3 of the Act. “The promoter/developer is hereby directed to register the project within two weeks from the date of this order.

news.title: Registered sale deed sacrosanct, rules ITAT
news.para: A registered sale deed holds significant legal weight, the Income Tax Appellate Tribunal in Chennai declared. The tribunal overturned an assessing officer's decision, which had denied a taxpayer's claim for long-term capital gains exemption. The officer had wrongly treated sale proceeds as unexplained money, despite a valid sale deed and reinvestment in a new property.

news.title: Godrej family buys two apartments in Mumbai's Worli for ₹294 crore
news.para: Godrej family scion Tanya Dubash has purchased two sea-facing luxury apartments in Mumbai's Worli for over Rs 294 crore, making it one of the country's most expensive residential deals. The ultra-luxury tower Naman Xana has attracted other high-profile buyers, highlighting continued demand for premium Mumbai residences as legacy assets.

news.title: Bajaj Electricals posts loss of ₹68 crore in Q4 FY26
news.para: Bajaj Electricals reported a consolidated net loss of Rs 67.53 crore for the March quarter of FY26, a significant downturn from a profit of Rs 59.05 crore in the previous year. This loss was attributed to a contraction in gross margin and the impact of new labour codes.

news.title: SC appointed panel says all deals involving Supertech Realtors' project need its approval
news.para: A Supreme Court committee now requires prior written approval for any property deals in Supertech Realtors' Noida 'Supernova' project. This applies to sales, transfers, and creation of third-party rights. Transactions without this approval will be void. The committee is working to revive the stalled project, with PwC appointed as Transaction Advisor. Homebuyers are urged to ensure compliance.
